growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (Bryant and Burkey mineral base) + 3 g/L glucose

up.units: mmol/gDW/h (not numerically reported; initial substrate 3 g/L consumed, time-course measured by Megazyme enzymatic kit)
up.method: measured (enzymatic assay of residual glucose over time); numeric qS not tabulated in text

up.units: normalized model flux (all product yields in Table 2/3 divided by glucose uptake = 1)
mmol/mmol glucose (max, model EFM boundary; consumed)
up.method: model EFM yield (Table 3, e7); ammonia also measured experimentally over time (Megazyme K-AMIAR)

up.units: supplied as 100% CO2 gas atmosphere; net consumed in some EFMs (e.g. glucose e1 -0.361 mmol/mmol)

sec.method: model EFM yield boundary (Table 2); acetate measured experimentally (Megazyme K-ACET) as a main product
model EFM yield boundary (Table 2); succinate measured experimentally (K-SUCC) as a main product

growth_records.cite: Fakih et al. Dynamic genome-based metabolic modeling of the predominant cellulolytic rumen bacterium Fibrobacter succinogenes S85. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01027-22. [PMC10308913]
growth_records.snippet: F. succinogenes strain S85 (ATCC 19169) was grown in triplicates in a chemically defined medium with 3 g/L glucose... The cultures were grown at 39C using Hungate tubes... under a 100% CO2 atmosphere. The simulated growth rate from F. succi
growth_records.notes: GEM/dynamic-modeling paper with real triplicate anaerobic batch cultures of F. succinogenes S85 on 3 g/L glucose, 39C, 100% CO2 (Hungate). Growth followed by OD600; products (succinate, acetate, formate), ammonia and glucose measured enzymatically at 0,5,9,13,17,20,24 h, but no summary experimental mu, qS or qP is tabulated (raw time-courses only in figures/supplement). growth_rate_per_h=0.137/h is the FBA-simulated whole-model max-biomass growth rate stated verbatim ('simulated growth rate ... is 0.137/hour'); doubling_time = ln2/0.137 = 0.693/0.137 = 5.06 h (calculated). Reduced core model reported as 72% higher (0.137*1.72=0.236/h). Dynamic-model calibrated Monod mu_max per EFM (Table 4, identifiability caveats flagged by authors) for glucose: e2=0.037, e5=0.031, e6=0.31, e9=0.004 /h. Product/biomass yields are model EFM yield boundaries from Table 2 (mmol product per mmol glucose; biomass in g/mmol). All uptake fluxes normalized so glucose=1. Ammonia max consumption 1.068 mmol/mmol from EFM e7 (Table 3). No O2 (strict anaerobe). pH not stated in text.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (Bryant and Burkey mineral base) + 3 g/L cellobiose

up.units: mmol/gDW/h (not numerically reported; initial 3 g/L; consumption tracked as reducing sugars by Miller's method)
up.method: measured (Miller reducing-sugar assay over time); numeric qS not tabulated

up.units: normalized model flux (product yields in Table 2/3 divided by cellobiose uptake=1; cellobiose treated as 2 glucose in model)
mmol/mmol cellobiose (max, model EFM boundary; consumed)
up.method: model EFM yield (Table 3, e1); ammonia also measured experimentally (K-AMIAR)

sec.method: model EFM yield boundary (Table 2); acetate measured experimentally (K-ACET), a main product
model EFM yield boundary (Table 2); succinate measured (K-SUCC), a main product
model EFM yield boundary (Table 2); formate measured (K-FORM), minor product

sec.units: mmol/mmol cellobiose (produced in EFM e1; consumed in others)

yields.Yxs_biomass_per_cellobiose_max: 0.2935 g biomass/mmol cellobiose (max model yield boundary, Table 2)

growth_records.cite: Fakih et al. Dynamic genome-based metabolic modeling of the predominant cellulolytic rumen bacterium Fibrobacter succinogenes S85. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01027-22. [PMC10308913]
growth_records.snippet: F. succinogenes strain S85 (ATCC 19169) was grown in triplicates in a chemically defined medium with 3 g/L glucose, cellobiose, or filter paper cellulose... grown at 39C ... under a 100% CO2 atmosphere. The bacterial growth on cellobiose or
growth_records.notes: Same organism/medium as glucose record but 3 g/L cellobiose carbon source; growth by OD600. No experimental summary mu/qS/qP tabulated. growth_rate_per_h=0.33/h is the dominant biomass-producing EFM (e1, biomass yield f=0.293, the highest) calibrated Monod mu_max from Table 4 (cellobiose EFMs e1=0.33, e4=0.0002, e5=0.033, e6=0.0001, e7=0.007 /h); doubling_time=ln2/0.33=0.693/0.33=2.1 h (calculated). Authors caution these Monod parameters have practical identifiability limits (K fixed at 9e-3 M). Product/biomass yields = model EFM yield boundaries (Table 2); cellobiose modeled as 2 glucose. Ammonia max uptake 2.181 mmol/mmol from EFM e1 (Table 3). Strict anaerobe (no O2). CVRMSE of dynamic model on cellobiose = 19%. pH not stated in text.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (Bryant and Burkey mineral base) + 3 g/L filter paper cellulose

up.units: particulate substrate; Contois kinetics used (Kc=6.05 mol cellulose/mol biomass); not expressed as mmol/gDW/h
up.method: model Contois kinetic (Table 4); cellulose consumption not directly assayed (growth tracked via succinate)

up.units: normalized model flux (product yields in Table 2/3 divided by cellulose uptake=1; cellulose treated as 4 glucose in model)
mmol/mmol cellulose (max, model EFM boundary; consumed)

sec.method: model EFM yield boundary (Table 2); acetate a main product
model EFM yield boundary (Table 2); succinate used to monitor growth on cellulose

sec.units: mmol/mmol cellulose (produced in EFM e2; consumed in others)

yields.Yxs_biomass_per_cellulose_max: 0.5801 g biomass/mmol cellulose (max model yield boundary, Table 2)

growth_records.cite: Fakih et al. Dynamic genome-based metabolic modeling of the predominant cellulolytic rumen bacterium Fibrobacter succinogenes S85. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01027-22. [PMC10308913]
growth_records.snippet: grown ... with 3 g/L glucose, cellobiose, or filter paper cellulose ... The quantification of succinate was used to monitor growth on cellulose cultures. Since cellulose is a particulate substrate, we modeled the macroscopic reactions using
growth_records.notes: Same organism/medium but 3 g/L filter-paper cellulose; particulate substrate modeled with Contois kinetics (Kc=6.05 mol cellulose/mol biomass), so no simple Monod mu_max is comparable. Dynamic-model per-EFM growth-rate constants (Table 4, cellulose): e1=0.087e-3, e3=0.34e-3, e6=0.96e-3, e8=0.08e-3 /h (Contois form; not directly a specific growth rate, so growth_rate_per_h left null to avoid misrepresentation). FBA whole-model growth rate 0.137/h reported for the network (see glucose record). Product/biomass yields = model EFM yield boundaries (Table 2); cellulose modeled as 4 glucose. Ammonia max uptake 4.311 mmol/mmol from EFM e2 (Table 3). Strict anaerobe. Dynamic-model CVRMSE on cellulose = 22% (succinate prediction poor). pH not stated.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 140659 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/140659
